label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about three different jars of low fat milk and what is true about them. The answer choices are A to D. The passage says that all three jars have the same amount of fat. The first says "1 percent fat," the second "99 percent fat free," and the third has "LOW FAT" in big letters and "1%" in small. The key part here is the line: "As you can see, all the milk has the same amount of fat. The milk is all the same. However, in this store the three jars of milk cost three different amounts of money. Maybe the customers will buy the milk that costs the most." So the question is asking which statement is correct based on this. Let's look at the options: A) one has more fat than the other two — this contradicts the passage which says all have the same fat. So A is wrong. B) they all cost the same amount of money — the passage says they cost different amounts. So B
